stem: Find the derivative of \( \arctan([[a]]x) \) with respect to \( x \), evaluated at \( x = [[x0]] \).
solution: By the chain rule \( \dfrac{d}{dx}\arctan([[a]]x) = \dfrac{[[a]]}{1 + ([[a]]x)^2} = \dfrac{[[a]]}{1 + [[a]]^2 x^2} \). At \( x = [[x0]] \) this is \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to square the coefficient \( [[a]] \) in the denominator.
This omits the chain-rule factor of \( [[a]] \) in the numerator.
This uses \( 1 - a^2x^2 \) in the denominator (wrong sign).
This inserts an extra factor of \( x \) into the numerator.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: Find the derivative of \( \arcsin(x) \) with respect to \( x \), evaluated at \( x = \dfrac{[[p]]}{[[q]]} \).
distractor_rationales: This uses \( \sqrt{1-x^2} \) (the reciprocal of the correct derivative).
This forgets the square root in the denominator.
This drops both the reciprocal and the square root.
This uses \( 1+x^2 \) under the root (wrong sign).
This has the wrong sign; the derivative of \( \arcsin \) is positive.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: Find the derivative of \( \arccos(x) \) with respect to \( x \), evaluated at \( x = \dfrac{[[p]]}{[[q]]} \).
distractor_rationales: This uses \( \sqrt{1-x^2} \) (the reciprocal of the correct derivative) with the wrong sign.
This drops the negative sign; the derivative of \( \arccos \) is always negative.
This forgets the square root in the denominator (but keeps the correct sign).
This uses \( 1+x^2 \) under the root and drops the negative sign.
This has the wrong sign; the derivative of \( \arccos \) is negative on \( (-1,1) \).
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: Let \( f(x) = x\arctan(x) \). Find \( f'([[x0]]) \) using the product rule.
solution: By the product rule, \( f'(x) = \arctan(x) + \dfrac{x}{1+x^2} \). At \( x = [[x0]] \) this is \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This only differentiates the \( \arctan(x) \) factor and forgets the other product term.
This only differentiates the \( x \) factor and forgets the \( \arctan(x) \) term.
This differentiates neither factor, just evaluates the original product.
This subtracts the two product-rule terms instead of adding them.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: Find the second derivative of \( \arctan(x) \) with respect to \( x \), evaluated at \( x = [[x0]] \).
solution: Since \( \dfrac{d}{dx}\arctan(x) = \dfrac{1}{1+x^2} \), differentiating again with the quotient rule gives \( \dfrac{d^2}{dx^2}\arctan(x) = \dfrac{-2x}{(1+x^2)^2} \). At \( x=[[x0]] \) this is \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This stops after the first derivative instead of differentiating a second time.
This forgets to square the full \( (1+x^2) \) denominator, expanding only \( (1+x)^2 \).
This drops the negative sign from the quotient rule.
This forgets the factor of 2 from differentiating \( x^2 \) in the denominator.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This has the wrong sign.

stem: Find the derivative of \( \arctan\!\left(\dfrac{[[a]]}{x}\right) \) with respect to \( x \), evaluated at \( x = [[x0]] \).
solution: By the chain rule with inner function \( u=\tfrac{[[a]]}{x} \), \( \dfrac{du}{dx}=-\tfrac{[[a]]}{x^2} \), so \( \dfrac{d}{dx}\arctan\!\left(\tfrac{[[a]]}{x}\right) = \dfrac{-[[a]]/x^2}{1+([[a]]/x)^2} = \dfrac{-[[a]]}{x^2+[[a]]^2} \). At \( x=[[x0]] \) this is \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This drops the negative sign from differentiating the reciprocal inner function.
This forgets the factor of \( [[a]] \) in the numerator entirely.
This uses \( x^2 - a^2 \) in the denominator (wrong sign inside).
This forgets to convert \( 1+(a/x)^2 \) to a single fraction, leaving \( x^2 \) out of the denominator.
This has the wrong sign.
This is off by one from the correct value.
